core_mechanism: construction of exact multi-soliton solutions of an integrable coupled nonlinear wave system by recasting its evolution equations into bilinear form and superposing exponential modes (Hirota's bilinearization), yielding localized coherent excitations whose pairwise collisions act as a nonlinear superposition rule producing amplitude-dependent phase shifts and amplitude/intensity redistribution across coupled components
brief_reason: The abstract states that mixed bright-dark soliton solutions of the integrable N-coupled equations are obtained via Hirota's bilinearization method, and that their collisions produce amplitude-dependent phase shifts and intensity redistribution among components, which is precisely the bilinear multi-soliton construction and its nonlinear collision superposition.
